framework.name: UN/IMAS EOD Training Framework
framework.reference: IMAS 09.31 IEDD, STM-EOD Unit Manual, FBI Hazardous Devices School curriculum

five_cs.name: CONFIRM
five_cs.description: Confirm threat via reconnaissance and intelligence. Never assume device type.

five_cs.name: CLEAR
five_cs.description: Clear non-essential personnel from blast radius. Establish minimum safe distance.

five_cs.name: CORDON
five_cs.description: Establish and maintain cordon. Control access points.

five_cs.name: CONTROL
five_cs.description: Designate incident commander. Maintain comms and accountability.

five_cs.name: CHECK
five_cs.description: Verify equipment, procedures, and team readiness before render-safe.

rsp_principles: Remote techniques are ALWAYS preferred over manual handling
No item is safe until fully disarmed — analyze the full explosive train
Interrupt firing circuit functions; do not rely on Hollywood wire-color rules alone
Maintain hands-off distance until remote tool is confirmed placed
Document every action for after-action report (AAR)

basic_rsp.description: Training module — circuit analysis based on observable indicators

advanced_ied.description: Anti-tamper circuit — cutting ANY wire without remote tool triggers BOoby trap

rsp_precision.description: Remote cutter accuracy on correct circuit
